metadata.title: The Last Testimony
metadata.author: AdventureKit
metadata.summary: An evidence-forward testimony prototype with investigation setup, witness dialogue, contradiction pressure, and a presentation hook.

items.name: Elevator Keycard
items.description: A keycard showing a basement elevator access at 11:42 p.m.

items.name: Torn Security Schedule
items.description: A ripped schedule that contradicts the guard's claimed post.

items.name: Raincoat Receipt
items.description: A receipt from the courthouse kiosk, timestamped minutes before the alarm.

scenes.title: Rain at the Courthouse
scenes.background: Stone steps reflecting blue security lights and late rain.

blocks.text: By morning, the trial has one witness left and one truth too many.

blocks.speaker: Ari Vale
blocks.text: We do not need the whole night. We need the one minute the witness keeps stepping around.

scenes.title: Basement Log
scenes.background: A humming records room with a single terminal awake in the dark.

blocks.speaker: Court Clerk
blocks.text: Nobody was meant to use the basement elevator after eleven.
The access record says someone did, and the keycard number belongs to the witness.

choices.label: Return to court with the keycard

scenes.title: Security Desk
scenes.background: A narrow desk stacked with umbrellas, thermal paper, and a silent radio.

blocks.speaker: Security Clerk
blocks.text: The guard swapped posts when the rain started. Said the east doors were leaking again.
A torn schedule places the witness away from the route he swore he never took.

scenes.title: Kiosk Receipt
scenes.background: A closed coffee kiosk beneath a maintenance light that buzzes like a held breath.

blocks.text: The receipt is small, damp, and decisive: a raincoat bought after the witness claimed he was already home.

scenes.title: Witness Stand
scenes.background: A courtroom washed in gray daylight, every surface too polished for comfort.

blocks.speaker: Witness
blocks.text: I left through the front doors before the alarm. I never went below the lobby.

blocks.speaker: Judge Ren
blocks.text: Counsel, your next question should matter.

hook.notes: Future module can let players select evidence and point to the exact line of testimony it contradicts.

scenes.title: Objection Sustained
scenes.background: The courtroom sound drops away after the gavel.

blocks.speaker: Judge Ren
blocks.text: The court will not follow speculation into the basement.
The witness exhales as if the floor has returned under him.

scenes.title: Witness Cracks
scenes.background: A bead of rainwater falls from the witness's sleeve onto the rail.

blocks.speaker: Witness
blocks.text: I only went down because I heard the alarm before it sounded upstairs.
The contradiction is not just where he was. It is what he knew too early.

endings.title: Timeline Revealed
endings.summary: The witness admits he knew about the alarm before it sounded, turning the case on a minute no one could explain.

endings.title: Thin Acquittal
endings.summary: The defense creates doubt, but the hidden route through the courthouse remains only half-lit.
